target.name: Skeleton Technologies
target.founder: Oliver Ahlberg, Taavi Madiberk, Jaan Leis, Anti Perkson

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Skeleton Technologies is a Series E frontier-tech company in the Climate & Energy sector, having raised €392M to date. The valuation universe consists of 9 comparable companies: 1 public (LNZA) and 8 private peers including TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, Helion, Commonwealth Fusion Systems, and QuantumScape. This peer set reflects the broader Climate & Energy sector landscape, though direct same-stage comparables are limited.

Peer-median methodology was deemed inapplicable due to insufficient narrow peer density. Of the 19 same-sector companies identified, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies yielded fewer than three datapoints—below the minimum threshold for reliable triangulation. An all-sector median approach risked introducing material bias given the heterogeneity of business models, technology readiness levels, and capital requirements across Climate & Energy subsegments.

The scarcity of tight peer comparables constrains traditional quantitative methods. Valuation support would require supplementary approaches beyond peer multiples, such as discounted cash flow analysis tied to company-specific unit economics and commercialization timelines, or venture capital method analysis anchored to realistic exit scenarios and probability-weighted returns. Key uncertainties include technology commercialization risk, regulatory pathway clarity, and capital intensity relative to projected revenue inflection. Without sufficient comparable transactions or earnings data, a defensible range cannot be established from the provided dataset alone.
